verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And they cast him out.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And Jesus heard that they had cast him out; and when He had found him, He said unto him, Dost thou believe on the Son of God?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He answered and said, Who is He, Lord, that I might believe on Him?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And the rest.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: They who for the sake of the truth and the confession of Christ suffer anything terrible and are insulted, these are especially honored.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For as he who loseth his possessions for His sake, the same it is who most findeth them; as he who hateth his own life, the same it is who most loveth it; so too he who is insulted, is the same who is most honored.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: As fell out in the case of the blind man.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The Jews cast him out from the Temple, and the Lord of the Temple found him; he was separated from that pestilent company, and met with the Fountain of salvation; he was dishonored by those who dishonored Christ, and was honored by the Lord of Angels.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Such are the prizes of truth.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And so we, if we leave our possessions in this world, find confidence in the next; if here we give to the afflicted, we shall have rest in heaven; if we be insulted for the sake of God, we are honored both here and there.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: When they had cast him out from the Temple, Jesus found him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The Evangelist shows, that He came for the purpose of meeting him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And observe how He recompenseth him, by that which is the chiefest of blessings.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For He made Himself known to him who before knew Him not, and enrolled him into the company of His own disciples.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Observe also how the Evangelist describes the exact circumstances; for when Christ had said, “Dost thou believe on the Son of God?” the man replied, “Lord, who is He?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For as yet he knew Him not, although he had been healed; because he was blind before he came to his Benefactor, and after the cure, he was being worried by those dogs.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Therefore, like some judge at the games, He receiveth the champion who had toiled much and gained the crown.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And what saith He?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Dost thou believe on the Son of God?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What is this, after so much arguing against the Jews, after so many words, He asketh him, “Dost thou believe?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He spake it not from ignorance, but desiring to make Himself known, and showing that He gently valued the man’s faith.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “This great multitude,” He saith, “hath insulted Me, but of them I make no account; for one thing I care, that thou shouldest believe.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For better is one who doeth the will of God, than ten thousand transgressors.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Dost thou believe on the Son of God?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: As having both been present, and as approving what had been said by him, He asketh this question; and first, He brought him to a state of longing for Himself.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For He said not directly, “Believe,” but in the way of an enquiry.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What then said the man?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Lord, who is He, that I might believe on Him?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The expression is that of a longing and enquiring soul.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He knoweth not Him in whose defense he had spoken so much, that thou mayest learn his love of truth.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For he had not yet seen Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Jesus saith unto him, Thou hast both seen Him, and it is He that talketh with thee.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He said not “I am He,” but as yet in an intermediate and reserved manner, “Thou hast both seen Him.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This was still uncertain; therefore He addeth more clearly, “It is He that talketh with thee.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “He saith, Lord, I believe; and he worshiped Him” (straightway).

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He said not, “I am He that healed thee, that bade thee, Go, wash in Siloam”; but keeping silence on all these points, He saith, “Dost thou believe on the Son of God?” and then the man, showing his great earnestness, straightway worshiped; which few of those who were healed had done; as, for instance, the lepers, and some others; by this act declaring His divine power.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For that no one might think that what had been said by him was a mere expression, he added also the deed.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: When he had worshiped, Christ said,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “For judgment I am come into the world, that they which see not might see, and that they which see might be made blind.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: So also saith Paul; “What shall we say then?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: That the Gentiles which followed not after righteousness have attained to righteousness, even the righteousness which is of the faith of Jesus; but Israel, which followed after the law of righteousness, hath not attained to the law of righteousness.” ( Rom. ix.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: By saying, “For judgment I am come into this world,” He both made the man stronger respecting the faith, and aroused those who followed Him; for the Pharisees were following Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And the, “For judgment,” He spake with reference to a greater punishment; showing that they who had given sentence against Him, had received sentence against themselves; that they who had condemned Him as a sinner, were themselves the persons condemned.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: In this passage He speaketh of two recoveries of sight, and two blindnesses; one sensible, the other spiritual.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Some of them that followed Him, say unto Him, Are we also blind?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: As in another place they said, “We were never servants to any man”; and, “We be not born of fornication” ( c. viii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 33, 41 ); so now they gape on material things alone, and are ashamed of this kind of blindness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Then to show that it was better for them to be blind than seeing, He saith,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “If ye were blind, ye should have no sin.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Since they deemed the calamity a matter to be ashamed of, He turneth this back upon their own head, telling them, that “this very thing would have rendered your punishment more tolerable”; cutting away on every side their human thoughts, and leading them to a notion high and marvelous.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “But now ye say, We see.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: As He saith in that other place, “Of whom ye said that He was your God” ( c. viii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 54 ); so too here, “Now ye say that ye see, but ye see not.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He showeth that what they deemed a great matter for praise, brought punishment upon them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He also comforted him who was blind from his birth, concerning his former maimed state, and then speaketh concerning their blindness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For He directeth His whole speech to this end, that they may not say, “We did not refuse to come to thee owing to our blindness, but we turn away and avoid thee as a deceiver.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And not without a cause hath the Evangelist mentioned, that they of the Pharisees who were with Him heard these things, and said, “Are we blind also?” but to remind thee that these were the men who first withdrew from and then stoned Him, for they were persons who followed Him superficially, and who easily changed to the contrary opinion.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: How then doth He prove that He is not a deceiver, but a Shepherd?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: By laying down the distinguishing marks both of the shepherd, and of him who is a deceiver and a spoiler, and from these affording them opportunity of searching into the truth of the matter.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And first He showeth who is a deceiver and a spoiler, calling him so from the Scriptures, and saying,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Chap. x. ver.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Verily, verily, I say unto you, he that entereth not by the door into the sheepfold, but climbeth up some other way, the same is a thief and a robber.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Observe the marks of a robber; first, that he doth not enter openly; secondly, not according to the Scriptures, for this is the, “not by the door.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Here also He referreth to those who had been before, and to those who should be after Him, Antichrist and the false Christs, Judas and Theudas, and whatever others there have been of the same kind.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And with good cause He calleth the Scriptures “a door,” for they bring us to God, and open to us the knowledge of God, they make the sheep, they guard them, and suffer not the wolves to come in after them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For Scripture, like some sure door, barreth the passage against the heretics, placing us in a state of safety as to all that we desire, and not allowing us to wander; and if we undo it not, we shall not easily be conquered by our foes.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: By it we can know all, both those who are, and those who are not, shepherds.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But what is “into the fold”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: It refers to the sheep, and the care of them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For he that useth not the Scriptures, but “climbeth up some other way,” that is, who cutteth out for himself another and an unusual way, “the same is a thief.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seest thou from this too that Christ agreeth with the Father, in that He bringeth forward the Scriptures?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: On which account also He said to the Jews, “Search the Scriptures” ( c. v.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 39 ); and brought forward Moses, and called him and all the Prophets witnesses, for “all,” saith He, “who hear the Prophets shall come to Me”; and, “Had ye believed Moses, ye would have believed Me.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But here He hath put the same thing metaphorically.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And by saying, “climbeth up some other way,” He alluded to the Scribes, because they taught for commandments the doctrines of men, and transgressed the Law ( Matt. xv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 9 ); with which He reproached them, and said, “None of you doeth the Law.” ( c. vii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Well did He say, “climbeth up,” not “entereth in,” since to climb is the act of a thief intending to overleap a wall, and who doeth all with danger.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Hast thou seen how He hath sketched the robber? now observe the character of the shepherd.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What then is it?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “He that entereth in by the door, the same is the shepherd of the sheep; to him the doorkeeper openeth, and the sheep hear his voice, and he calleth his own by name.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And when he hath brought them out, he goeth before them.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He hath set down the marks of the shepherd, and of the evil doer; let us now see how He hath fitted to them what followeth.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “To him,” He saith, “the doorkeeper openeth”; He continueth in the metaphor to make the discourse more emphatic.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But if thou shouldest be minded to examine the parable word by word, there is nothing to hinder thee from supposing Moses to be the doorkeeper, for to him were entrusted the oracles of God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Whose voice the sheep hear, and he calleth his own by name.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Because they everywhere said that He was a deceiver, and confirmed this by their own unbelief, saying, “Which of the rulers hath believed on him?” ( c. vii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He showeth that they ought not on account of the unbelief of those persons to call Him a spoiler and deceiver, but that they, because they gave no heed to Him were consequently even excluded from the rank of sheep.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For if a shepherd’s part is to enter through the usual door, and if He entered through this, all they who followed Him might be sheep, but they who rent themselves away, hurt not the reputation of the Shepherd, but cast themselves out from the kindred of the sheep.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And if farther on He saith that He is “the door,” we must not again be disturbed, for He also calleth Himself “Shepherd,” and “Sheep,” and in different ways proclaimeth His dispensations.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Thus, when He bringeth us to the Father, He calleth Himself “a Door,” when He taketh care of us, “a Shepherd”; and it is that thou mayest not suppose, that to bring us to the Father is His only office, that He calleth Himself a Shepherd.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And the sheep hear his voice, and he calleth his own sheep, and leadeth them out, and goeth before them.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Shepherds indeed do the contrary, for they follow after them; but He to show that He will lead all men to the truth, doeth differently; as also when He sent the sheep, He sent them, not out of the way of wolves, but “in the midst of wolves.” ( Matt. x.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For far more wonderful is this manner of keeping sheep than ours.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He seemeth to me also to allude to the blind man, for him too, having “called,” He “led out” from the midst of the Jews, and the man heard “His voice,” and “knew” it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And a stranger will they not follow, for they know not the voice of strangers.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Certainly here He speaketh of Theudas and Judas, (for “all, as many as believed on them, were scattered” [ Acts v.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 36], It saith,) or of the false Christs who after that time should deceive.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For lest any should say that He was one of these, He in many ways separateth Himself from them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And the first difference He setteth down is His teaching from the Scriptures; for He by means of these led men to Him, but the others did not from these draw men after them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The second is, the obedience of the sheep; for on Him they all believed, not only while He lived, but when He had died; the others they straightway left.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: With these we may mention a third difference, no trifling one.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: They did all as rebels, and to cause revolts, but He placed Himself so far from such suspicion, that when they would have made Him a king, He fled; and when they asked, “Is it lawful to give tribute unto Cæsar?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He bade them pay it, and Himself gave the two drachm piece. ( Matt. xvii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Besides this, He indeed came for the saving of the sheep, “That they might have life, and that they might have more abundantly” ( ver.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 10 ), but the others deprived them even of this present life.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: They betrayed those who were entrusted to them and fled, but He withstood so nobly as even to give up His life.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: They unwillingly, and by compulsion, and desiring to escape, suffered what they suffered, but He willingly and by choice endured all.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “This parable spake Jesus unto them, but they understood not what things they were which He spake unto them.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And wherefore spake He obscurely?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Because He would make them more attentive; when He had effected this, He removes the obscurity, saying,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “I am the door, by Me if any man enter in, he shall go in and out, and find pasture.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: As though He had said, “shall be in safety and security,” (but by “pasture,” He here meaneth His nurturing and feeding the sheep, and His power and Lordship,) that is, “shall remain within, and none shall thrust him out.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Which took place in the case of the Apostles, who came in and went out securely, as having become lords of all the world, and none was able to cast them out.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “All that ever came before Me are thieves and robbers, but the sheep did not hear them.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He doth not here speak of the Prophets, (as the heretics assert,) for as many as believed on Christ did hear them also, and were persuaded by them; but of Theudas and Judas, and the other exciters of sedition.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Besides, He saith, “the sheep did not hear them,” as praising them; now nowhere is He seen to praise those who refused to hearken to the Prophets, but, on the contrary, to reproach and accuse them vehe mently; whence it is evident that the, “did not hear,” refers to those leaders of sedition.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “The thief cometh not but for to steal, and to kill, and to destroy.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Which then took place when all (their followers) were slain and perished.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “But I am come that they might have life, and that they might have more.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And what is “more” than life, tell me?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The kingdom of heaven.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But He doth not as yet say this, but dwelleth on the name of “life,” which was known to them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “I am the good Shepherd.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Here He next speaketh concerning the Passion, showing that this should be for the salvation of the world, and that He came to it not unwillingly.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Then again He mentioneth the character of the shepherd and the hireling.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “For the shepherd layeth down his life.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “But he that is an hireling and not a shepherd, whose own the sheep are not, seeth the wolf coming, and leaveth the sheep and fleeth, and the wolf cometh and catcheth them.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Here He declareth Himself to be Master even as the Father, if so be that He is the Shepherd, and the sheep are His.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seest thou how He speaketh in a more lofty tone in His parables, where the sense is concealed; and giveth no open handle to the listeners?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What then doth this hireling?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He “seeth the wolf coming, and leaveth the sheep, and the wolf cometh, and scattereth them.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This those false teachers did, but He the contrary.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For when He was taken, He said, “Let these go their way, that the saying might be fulfilled” ( c. xviii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 8, 9 ), that not one of them was lost.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Here also we may suspect a spiritual wolf to be intended; for neither did Christ allow him to go and seize the sheep.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But he is not a wolf only, but a lion also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Because our adversary the devil,” It saith, “walketh about as a roaring lion.” ( 1 Pet. v.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He is also a serpent, and a dragon; for, “Tread ye on serpents and scorpions.” ( Luke x.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore, I beseech you, let us remain pasturing beneath this Shepherd; and we shall remain, if we obey Him, if we hear His voice, if we follow not a stranger.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And what is His voice?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Blessed are the poor in spirit, blessed are the pure in heart, blessed are the merciful.” ( Matt. v.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: If thus we do, we shall remain beneath the Shepherd, and the wolf will not be able to come in; or if he come against us, he will do so to his own hurt.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For we have a Shepherd who so loveth us, that He gave even His life for us.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: When therefore He is both powerful and loveth us, what is there to hinder us from being saved?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Nothing, unless we ourselves revolt from Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And how can we revolt?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Hear Him saying, “Ye cannot serve two masters, God and mammon.” ( Matt. vi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: If then we serve God, we shall not submit to the tyranny of mammon.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And truly a bitterer thing than any tyranny is the desire of riches; for it brings no pleasure, but cares, and envyings, and plottings, and hatred, and false accusations, and ten thousand impediments to virtue, indolence, wantonness, greediness, drunkenness, which make even freemen slaves, nay, worse than slaves bought with money, slaves not to men, but even to the most grievous of the passions, and maladies of the soul.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Such a one dares many things displeasing to God and men, dreading lest any should remove from him this dominion.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: O bitter slavery, and devlish tyranny!

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For this is the most grievous thing of all, that when entangled in such evils we are pleased and hug our chain, and dwelling in a prison house full of darkness, refuse to come forth to the light, but rivet evil upon ourselves, and rejoice in our malady.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: So that we cannot be freed, but are in a worse state than those that work the mines, enduring labors and affliction, but not enjoying the fruit.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And what is in truth worse than all, if any one desire to free us from this bitter captivity, we do not suffer it, but are even vexed and displeased, being in this respect in no better case than madmen, or rather in a much more miserable state than any such, inasmuch as we are not even willing to be delivered from our madness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What? was it for this, O man, that thou wast brought into the world?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Was it for this that thou wast made a man, that thou mightest work in these mines, and gather gold?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Not for this did God create thee in His Image, but that thou mightest please Him, that thou mightest obtain the things to come, that thou mightest join the choir of Angels.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Why now dost thou banish thyself from such a relationship, and thrust thyself into the extreme of dishonor and meanness?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He who came by the same birth pangs with thee, (the spiritual birth pangs I mean,) is perishing with hunger, and thou art bursting with fullness: thy brother goeth about with naked body, but thou providest garments even for thy garments, heaping up all this clothing for the worms.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: How much better would it have been to put them on the bodies of the poor; so would they have remained undestroyed, would have freed thee from all care, and have won for thee the life to come.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: If thou wilt not have them to be moth-eaten, give them to the poor, these are they who know how to shake these garments well.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The Body of Christ is more precious and more secure than the coffer, for not only doth It keep the garments safe, not only doth It preserve them unconsumed, but even rendereth them brighter.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Oftentimes the coffer taken with the garments causeth thee the utmost loss, but this place of safety not even death can harm.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: With It we need neither doors nor bolts, nor wakeful servants, nor any other such security, for our possessions are free from all treacherous attacks, and are laid up under guard, as we may suppose things laid up in heaven would be; for to all wickedness that place is inaccessible.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: These things we cease not continually to say to you, and you hearing are not persuaded.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The reason is, that we are of a soul which is mean, gaping upon the earth, groveling on the ground.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Or rather, God forbid that I should condemn you all of wickedness, as though all were incurably diseased.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For even if those who are drunk with riches stop their ears against my words, yet they who live in poverty will be able to look clearly to what I say.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “But what,” saith some one, “hath this to do with the poor? for they have no gold, or any such garments.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: No, but they have bread and cold water, but they have two obols, and feet to visit the sick, but they have a tongue and speech to comfort the bedridden, but they have house and shelter to make the stranger their inmate.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: We demand not from the poor such and such a number of talents of gold, these we ask from the rich.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But if a man be poor, and come to the doors of others, our Lord is not ashamed to receive even an obol, but will say that He hath received more from the giver, than from those who cast in much.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: How many of those who now stand here would desire to have been born at that time, when Christ went about the earth in the flesh, to have conversed and sat at meat with Him?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Lo, this may be done now, we may invite Him more than then to a meal, and feast with Him, and that to greater profit.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For of those who then feasted with Him many even perished, as Judas and others like him; but every one of those who invite Him to their houses now, and share with Him table and roof, shall enjoy a great blessing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Come,” it saith, “ye blessed of My Father, inherit the Kingdom prepared for you from the foundation of the world.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For I was an hungered, and ye gave Me meat: I was thirsty, and ye gave Me drink: I was a stranger, and ye took Me in; sick, and ye visited Me; I was in prison, and ye came unto Me.” ( Matt. xxv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: That then we may hear these words, let us clothe the naked, let us bring in the stranger, feed the hungry, give the thirsty drink, let us visit the sick, and look upon him that is in prison, that we may have boldness and obtain remission of our sins, and share those good things which transcend both speech and thought.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Which may we all obtain, through the grace and lovingkindness of our Lord Jesus Christ, to whom be the glory and the might forever.
